content: What is the central confession of faith in Deuteronomy 6 called?
explanation: Deuteronomy 6:4-5 — 'Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.'

content: What was the purpose of the 40 years in the wilderness according to Deuteronomy 8?
explanation: Deuteronomy 8:2 — To humble and test you, to know what was in your heart.
options: To humble, test, and know what was in their heart

content: What does God require of Israel according to Deuteronomy 10:12?
explanation: Deuteronomy 10:12-13 — A summary of what the LORD requires.
options: Fear, walk in His ways, love, serve, and keep His commandments

content: What does Deuteronomy 30 call for choosing?
explanation: Deuteronomy 30:19 — I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse; therefore choose life.

content: What does Deuteronomy 4 emphasize would make other nations praise Israel as wise?
explanation: Deuteronomy 4:7-8 — What nation has its God so near, and statutes so righteous?
options: The righteous law and the nearness of God

content: Why must the high places be destroyed and worship only happen at the place God chooses?
explanation: Deuteronomy 12:2-14 — Tear down the high places; seek the place the LORD chooses to put His Name.
options: To prevent idolatry and unite worship

content: Why are signs alone insufficient to accept a prophet?
explanation: Deuteronomy 13:1-3 — Even if a sign occurs, if it leads to other gods, do not listen.
options: Because we must obey the king
If it leads to other gods, it must be rejected

content: What does the year of debt release (Shemitah) teach?
explanation: Deuteronomy 15:1-11 — Every seven years cancel debts; have no evil heart.

content: What limits does the law of the king in Deuteronomy 17 set?
explanation: Deuteronomy 17:16-17 — Limits on power so the king fears the LORD.
options: No multiplying horses, wives, silver, or gold

content: In context, to whom does 'a Prophet like Moses' first refer?
explanation: Deuteronomy 18:15 — God will raise up for you a prophet like me.
options: The line of later prophets, ultimately fulfilled in Christ

content: How is one hanged on a tree regarded according to Deuteronomy 21:23?
explanation: Deuteronomy 21:23 — A hanged man is cursed by God (Paul applies this in Galatians 3:13).

content: What does the gleaning law reveal about God's heart?
explanation: Deuteronomy 24:19-22 — Leave the gleanings for the poor and the sojourner.
options: Concern for the poor, sojourner, fatherless, widow

content: What does 'The secret things belong to the LORD... but the things revealed belong to us' teach?
explanation: Deuteronomy 29:29 — We are responsible for what has been revealed.
options: Limits of revelation and the duty of obedience

content: On what foundation does Deuteronomy 4:32-40 ground the uniqueness of God?
explanation: Deuteronomy 4 — History and revelation experience confirm that God is one.
options: Salvation history and revelation at Sinai

content: What does 'Circumcise the foreskin of your heart' mean?
explanation: Deuteronomy 10:16 — Be no longer stubborn; an image of inner transformation.
options: Set the heart apart for God; cease being stiff-necked

content: What test distinguishes a true from a false prophet according to Deuteronomy 18?
explanation: Deuteronomy 18:22 and 13:1-3 — Two criteria: fulfillment and faithfulness to the LORD.
options: The word comes to pass and does not lead to other gods

content: What do the restrictions on entering the assembly of the LORD in Deuteronomy 23 reflect?
explanation: Deuteronomy 23:1-8 — Boundaries shaped by holiness theology and history with Ammon and Moab.
options: The holiness of the assembly and a hostile history

content: What does the curse ceremony at Mount Ebal and Gerizim emphasize?
explanation: Deuteronomy 27:11-26 — The curses pronounced for those who do not obey.

content: What is the chief purpose of the Song of Moses (Deuteronomy 32)?
explanation: Deuteronomy 32 — A song to bear witness to Israel about her sinful path and God's grace.
options: Witness covenant indictment, warn of unfaithfulness, promise restoration

content: How does Deuteronomy 34 conclude regarding Moses?
explanation: Deuteronomy 34:10-12 — There has not arisen a prophet since like Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face.
options: No prophet like Moses, whom the LORD knew face to face

content: How does the Shema — Judaism's central declaration of faith — begin?
explanation: Deuteronomy 6:4 — The Shema is the central creed: 'The LORD our God, the LORD is one.'
options: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one

content: According to Deuteronomy 6:5, with how many things must we love the LORD?
explanation: Deuteronomy 6:5 — You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your might. Jesus called this the greatest commandment.
options: With all the heart, strength, and mind
With all the heart and soul
With all the heart, soul, and might

content: When Moses said, 'The LORD will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers,' of whom did he prophesy?
explanation: Acts 3:22-23 — Peter affirms this prophecy is fulfilled in Jesus, the greatest Prophet.

content: Why did God feed the people manna in the wilderness?
explanation: Deuteronomy 8:3 — Jesus quoted this verse when tempted by Satan (Matthew 4:4).
options: To teach that man does not live by bread alone but by every word from God

content: Why did God choose the people of Israel?
explanation: Deuteronomy 7:7-8 — God chose Israel not because they were many or great, but because of His love and the covenant with the patriarchs.
options: Because of His love and promise to the fathers, not because of size

content: What does Deuteronomy 10:12 say the LORD requires of His people?
explanation: Deuteronomy 10:12 — A summary of the entire law in one verse: fear, love, and serve the LORD.
options: Fear the LORD, walk in His ways, love and serve Him with all your heart

content: What does Moses teach the people to do with the Word of God?
explanation: Deuteronomy 11:18-20 — God's Word should saturate every aspect of life: personal, family, society.
options: Keep it locked in the Ark of the Covenant
Bind on hand, on forehead, teach children, write on doors

content: Whom does Deuteronomy 28 promise blessings to?
explanation: Deuteronomy 28:1-2 — If you obey the voice of the LORD, all blessings will come upon you and overtake you.
options: The one who obeys the voice of the LORD

content: What does Deuteronomy 29:29 distinguish?
explanation: Deuteronomy 29:29 — Some things God keeps hidden, and others He reveals. We are responsible for what is revealed.
options: Secret things belong to the LORD; things revealed belong to us

content: What does Moses call the people to choose?
explanation: Deuteronomy 30:19 — Moses sets before the people two paths: life and death, blessing and curse. Choose life!
options: Life and blessing (rather than death and curse)

content: How does Deuteronomy 33:27 describe God?
explanation: Deuteronomy 33:27 — The eternal God is your dwelling place, and underneath are the everlasting arms.
options: Eternal dwelling place; underneath the everlasting arms

content: At what age did Moses die?
explanation: Deuteronomy 34:7 — Moses lived to 120. His eye was undimmed and his vigor unabated.
